description_long: This is a medium-sized, open-topped cardboard box with four flaps, two of which are fully open and two partially open, revealing the interior. The box is constructed from brown corrugated cardboard, with a slightly darker brown inner lining visible along the top edge. The exterior surfaces are smooth and unmarked, with no visible labels, tape, or branding. The box is rectangular in shape, with straight, vertical sides and a flat base, designed for holding or transporting items. The flaps are creased for easy folding and closing, and the box appears empty, with air occupying the interior. This type of box is commonly used for shipping, moving, or storing parcels in urban environments such as sidewalks, garages, or delivery areas.
description: A brown, open cardboard box with four unfolded flaps and a visible empty interior, made for holding or transporting items. The box is unmarked, rectangular, and constructed from corrugated cardboard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open box with all four flaps visible, the two side flaps angled outward and the front and back flaps partially open.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the right side of the box, with the right flap more prominent and a small portion of the box's side edge visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with the back flap and both side flaps visible, showing a clean, uninterrupted surface with minimal side detail.
description_view_3: Left-side view with the left flap more prominent and a small section of the box's bottom edge visible, similar to the right-side view but mirrored.
